name: key-vault
context: Create Key Vaults, assign RBAC, and manage secret values with `az keyvault secret`
info: Azure Key Vault is the secret boundary for Azure-hosted workloads and deployment automation. Agents use this skill to create vaults, grant the right role assignment for secret operations, set or rotate secret values, retrieve them when absolutely necessary, and delete or purge secrets during cleanup. The CLI surface is `az keyvault` plus ordinary Azure RBAC role assignment commands.
triggers: store a secret in key vault
read a secret from azure key vault
set a secret with az keyvault
body:
# Azure Key Vault

Key Vault secret operations are a combination of two surfaces: create the vault itself, then ensure the executing identity has a role such as `Key Vault Secrets Officer` at the vault scope before attempting secret writes or reads.

~~~bash
# Create a vault with RBAC-based authorization
az keyvault create \
  --name kv-dojo-prod \
  --resource-group rg-dojo-shared \
  --location eastus \
  --enable-rbac-authorization true

# Grant secret-management rights to a principal
az role assignment create \
  --role "Key Vault Secrets Officer" \
  --assignee "$AZURE_ASSIGNEE" \
  --scope "$(az keyvault show --name kv-dojo-prod --query id -o tsv)"

# Set and retrieve a secret
az keyvault secret set --vault-name kv-dojo-prod --name OPENAI_API_KEY --value "$OPENAI_API_KEY"
az keyvault secret show --vault-name kv-dojo-prod --name OPENAI_API_KEY --query value -o tsv

# Delete or purge when cleanup is required
az keyvault secret delete --vault-name kv-dojo-prod --name OPENAI_API_KEY
az keyvault secret purge --vault-name kv-dojo-prod --name OPENAI_API_KEY
~~~

Use Key Vault as the source of truth for sensitive settings consumed by [[azure/app-service]] or [[azure/functions]]. Reading plaintext secret values should be the exception, not the normal control flow. Prefer managed identity and configuration indirection where the runtime can fetch or reference secrets without exposing them back into logs or CI output.

sections.title: Create vaults and enable RBAC
sections.body: `az keyvault create --enable-rbac-authorization true` creates the vault and leaves authorization in Azure RBAC instead of legacy access policies. That makes permission handling consistent with the rest of Azure automation and easier to reason about from resource IDs and role scopes.

sections.title: Grant vault-scoped permissions
sections.body: A principal must have an appropriate role on the vault scope before secret operations succeed. Microsoft Learn's quickstart uses `az role assignment create --role "Key Vault Secrets Officer" --assignee ... --scope <vault-id>` as the concrete CLI path. Keep the scope narrow to the specific vault whenever possible.

sections.title: Set, show, delete, and purge secrets
sections.body: `az keyvault secret set` creates or updates a secret, `show` returns the current version, `delete` soft deletes it, and `purge` permanently removes it. Agents should be extremely careful with `show --query value -o tsv` because it converts the secret into plaintext output that can leak into logs.

links.context: Authenticate as a principal that can receive or use vault-scoped RBAC
Create the shared or workload-specific group before the vault
Use vault-backed secrets instead of hard-coded web app settings
Store sensitive Function App settings outside deployment manifests
Inspect control-plane failures when RBAC or vault operations are rejected

related.note: Secret operations require the right principal and RBAC scope
App Service often consumes secrets stored in Key Vault
Function Apps frequently externalize secrets into Key Vault

scripts.name: Manage Key Vault
scripts.description: Create a Key Vault, grant RBAC, and manage secret values with Azure CLI

AZURE_KEY_VAULT_ACTION.description: One of vault-create, grant-secrets-officer, secret-set, secret-show, secret-delete, or secret-purge

AZURE_KEY_VAULT_ASSIGNEE.description: User, group, or service principal to grant the Key Vault Secrets Officer role

AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID.description: Subscription ID or name to activate before the command

assignee.description: Principal to grant the Key Vault Secrets Officer role

id.description: ARM resource ID for the vault or secret

depends.reason: Vault creation and secret operations require a valid Azure identity with RBAC
Vaults are created inside a resource group
The execution wrapper is implemented in Bash

author: dojo-community
